betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Oil Refinery§f is the core of petroleum processing. Takes §bhot oil§f and splits it into various fractions based on boiling points. §aPurely thermodynamic§f - requires no electricity, just hot input fluid.

§eSetup:§f
• Crafted in §bAssembly Machine§f (Steel plates, copper, pipes, vacuum tubes)
• First heat oil in §bIndustrial Boiler§f to "Hot" state
• Pipe hot oil into Refinery

§eHot Crude Oil (1000mB) yields:§f
• §b300mB Heavy Oil§f → Lubricant or crack to lighter fractions
• §b350mB Naphtha§f → Gasoline production
• §b200mB Light Oil§f → Diesel fuel
• §b150mB Unsaturated Hydrocarbons§f
• Byproduct: §bParaffin Wax§f

§eHot Cracked Oil (1000mB) yields:§f
• 350mB Naphtha
• 350mB Light Oil
• 150mB Aromatic Hydrocarbons
• 150mB Unsaturated Hydrocarbons
• Byproduct: Paraffin Wax

§eDesulfurized variants§f produce desulfurized outputs + §bSulfur§f byproduct.

§aTips:§f
• §aNo power needed§f - fuel cost is in the Boiler
• Repair with §bBlowtorch§f and §bSteel Plates§f if damaged
• The letters on facets spell "Fluttershy" (MLP reference)
